1.1 SCOPE  
The purpose of this document is to cover the design, detail engineering, procurement and supply of 
material, fabrication, assembly, expediting and inspection, testing, painting, packing, marking, 
guarantees, performances, documentation, certification and permits of the following API 610 Type 
medium duty process centrifugal Pumps: 
BASE CASE to be included in the base scope of supply: 
Equipment

(HOLD)

51-P-2002 A/BRecycle Column Reflux 
Pump  2 2 Zone 2, IIA T3 
(HOLD)  
51-P-2004 A/BButene-1 Column Reflux 
Pump  2 2 Zone 2, IIA T3 
(HOLD)  
51-P-2006 A/B C5+ Product Drum Pump 2 2 Zone 2, IIA T3 
(HOLD)  
51-P-2007
A/B/CButene-1 Product Pump 3 2 Zone 2, IIA T3 
(HOLD)  
56-P-7001 A/BHP Flare Knock-Out Drum 
Pump  2 2 Zone 2, IIA T3 
(HOLD)  
NOTE: For all items mentioned above Instruments shall be certified as minimum for Zone 1 IIC T3  
Including all equipment and ancillaries as hereinafter specified, in accordance with the requirements 
set forth in this Supply Specification and referenced documents. 
The above listed equipment will be installed in the “First Integrated Gas Chemical Complex. Second 
phase (Polyethylene production)” in the Karabatan, Kazakhstan, located 32 km to the north-east of 
Atyrau city on the territory of the special economic zone (SEZ)  as part of the SILLENO  Project. 
Th
e Equipment part of this Supply Specification shall be designed for a service life of 25 years and a 
minimum site turnaround interval of 5 years.

chunks.heading: 1.3 ORDER OF PRECEDENCE
chunks.content:
1.3 ORDER OF PRECEDENCE  
In case of conflict among the requirement of this specification and other documents applicable to this 
project, VENDOR shall consider the following rank of precedence: 
Licensor standards (where foreseen)
Local laws and regulations
Agreed Technical Deviations
The Material Requisition
Project General Specifications
Project Standards
International Codes and Standard.
Other referenced documentation
As a general guideline, the most stringent requirements will govern. However, VENDOR  shall inform 
CONTRACTOR about the discovered conflict to solve the inconsistency. 
Conflict resolution and / or interpretation, based on mentioned order of precedence, shall be obtained 
from CONTRACTOR in writing for VENDOR to proceed with relevant due action. 
CONTRACTOR will not be held responsible of any consequence out-coming from misinterpretation, 
VENDOR’s standard data proposed by VENDOR on revised technical offer or VENDOR’s filled in 
data table (.xls file) tabulation, technical clarification, signed MR documents etc which after issuance 
of PO found non-compliance or inconsistent within various project specifications attached with MR or 
non-compliance with above indicated instructions, there including any action should be required on 
VENDOR’s side to fully comply with the contractual obligations without commercial impact on 
CONTRACTOR/OWNER.

chunks.heading: 1.5 REFERENCES LIST
chunks.content:
1.5 REFERENCES LIST 
BIDDER shall submit with proposal a reference list demonstrating his experience in manufacturing 
proposed equipment under similar operating/design conditions.  
The BIDDER shall have manufactured at least 2 similar equipment running under similar service 
conditions over a period of at least five (5) years demonstrating good operation and reliability. Prototype 
design/machines are not acceptable.

chunks.heading: 1.6 ALTERNATIVE DESIGN
chunks.content:
1.6 ALTERNATIVE DESIGN
Base quotation shall be based on the prescriptions of this Supply Specification. Additionally, BIDDER  
can propose alternative solutions, more reliable from technical perspective and more competitive from 
the commercial point of view and without any reduction of the supply quality. BIDDER shall not take 
into consideration alternate proposal unless approved by LICENSOR/ CONTRACTOR/ OWNER.

chunks.heading: 4.2 LANGUAGE
chunks.content:
4.2 LANGUAGE  
VENDOR shall use English language for all correspondence and communications and in general for 
the execution of the Purchase Order.  
All drawings and documents submitted by VENDOR for CONTRACTOR’ s review and approval shall 
be in English Language till the final approval. Once the drawings and documents will be approved as 
final with no comments, they must be translated also in Russian language. 
IOM, TR CU and Technical Passports must be in English, Russian and Kazakh language as required 
by the local rules.

chunks.heading: 4.3 SITE CONDITIONS
chunks.content:
4.3 SITE CONDITIONS
For site environment conditions VENDOR shall refer to document 4503-YZ-SG-T0000001 (SLN-2725-
0000-00-A-0001) “Basic Engineering Design Data”. 
The mechanical equipment and components will be installed outdoor, under shelter and shall be 
designed for the specified outdoor design conditions. 
MDMT = -38°C.

chunks.heading: 4.4 WINTERIZATION /TROPICALIZATION
chunks.content:
4.4 WINTERIZATION /TROPICALIZATION  
Winterization/Tropicalization shall be provided for all fluids that can potentially freeze/overheat during 
cold/hot ambient temperatures, including utilities. 
VENDOR shall consider the following features for winterization/tropicalization: 
- equipment suitable for site minimum/maximum design temperature.
- appropriate oil system heat-tracing/cold/hot weather protection, if applicable.
- appropriate shaft seal system heat-tracing/cold/hot weather protection, if applicable.
- heat/cold tracing and insulation to prevent icing/overheat of all draining facilities
from equipment handling fluid prone to freeze.
- heat/Cold tracing and insulation of equipment including auxiliary piping on the skid, if
applicable.
- instruments suitable for low/high ambient temperature.
- heating Box / sun shelter for the instrument itself and the hook up.
- Heat/Cold tracing and insulation for instrumentation and hook up, low points and dead legs
where water and/or liquids prone to freeze can settle.

chunks.heading: 4.5 UTILITIES CONDITIONS
chunks.content:
Page  10 of 57 4.5 UTILITIES CONDITIONS  
For available utilities characteristics at the equipment battery limit refer to document 4503-YZ-SG-
T0000002 (SLN-2725-0000-00-A-0002) “Utility and Auxiliary Materials Conditions and Specifications”. 
Utility conditions indicated in above mentioned document are at plant battery limit. Correct value 
available at package location must be discussed during technical alignment. 
Utilities like Instrument Air, Plant Air and Nitrogen could reach the package inlet with a minimum 
temperature of minus 38 °C. 
Vendor must identify and list the items/parts/skids which do not withstand minus 38 °C due to 
mechanical reasons. When items are identified, minimum acceptable temperature shall be defined for 
each of these items/parts/skids. 
Vendor must check if a suitable solution (like online electrical heaters to be installed at the inlet of each 
skid for each utility) can be provided and quote it as option.

chunks.heading: 5 HEALTH, SAFETY AND ENVIRONMENT (H.S.E.)
chunks.content:
5 HEALTH, SAFETY AND ENVIRONMENT (H.S.E.) 
Design studies, material supply and construction activities shall comply with all the H.S.E aspects of 
the project. VENDOR shall demonstrate his own H.S.E commitment (H.S.E policy, Design HSE Plan). 
VENDOR shall ensure that the design, fabrication, and testing of his equipment does not endanger 
the health and safety of his own employees, employees of his SUB-VENDOR, employees of site 
erection CONTRACTOR, and future operators of the Plant. 
VENDOR shall provide necessary recommendations for a safe installation, operation, and 
maintenance of his equipment. Equipment emissions shall comply with the local environmental 
regulations. 
VENDOR  shall be responsible for ensuring that equipment/package meet all applicable regulations 
on health, safety, and environmental issues.  
HAZOP (HAZard and OPerability) study and SIL (Safety Integrity Level) classification reviews will be 
performed during detailed engineering phase. 
Upon CONTRACTOR’s or OWNER’ s decision, VENDOR  may be required to participate to such 
sessions, which shall be part of his contractual obligations.  
HAZOP & SIL Review participation & consequent implementation of the implications shall be in 
VENDOR’s scope. No cost and time implication shall be entertained by VENDOR in relation to 
HAZOP/SIL result after order placement.

for each activity.

chunks.heading: 10.2 PIPING
chunks.content:
10.2 PIPING  
The packaged equipment layout shall meet this specification in terms of required operation and 
maintenance accessibility and lifting facilities.  
Piping shall be routed considering the insulation thickness, equipment accessibility and 
maintainability requirements. 
The tie- in points on packaged equipment shall be brought to the edge of the skid and terminate with 
flanges.

Page  21 of 57 Piping installed on skid shall be delivered welded, mounted, tested and painted.  
Flanged connections shall be used. Threaded connections are not allowed for process pipe. 
Welded connections shall be butt-welded, except on non-hazardous and non-flammable fluids. 
Connections at VENDOR’s scope limit shall be flanged as per the relevant CONTRACTOR’s piping 
class. 
On-skid piping shall be run to the edge of the base plate and shall terminate with a flange. 
All nozzles provided on casings, vessels, headers, piping, etc. shall be NPS ¾” minimum. 
Non-Standard Sizes like 1¼”, 2½”, 3½”, 5”, 7”, 9”, 22”, 26”, etc. shall not be used. 
All necessary pressure safety valves shall be identified and defined by the VENDOR. Unless 
otherwise specified in the scope of supply, all necessary pressure safety valves shall be included in 
VENDOR's scope. 
For equipment/components weighing more than 20kg, crane access or, alternatively, davits shall be 
provided. 
Piping shall be laid out so that equipment such as control valves, relief valves, start-up strainers, pump 
casings and shafts, heat exchanger bundles, compressor cartridges/ pistons, etc., can be removed 
with a minimum amount of dismantling and without providing additional supports. 
Platforms shall be provided for valves or equipment which cannot be reached from grade level. 
Piping shall be arranged to eliminate overhead obstructions and tripping hazards. Minimum clear 
headroom for overhead piping shall be 2.2m. 
Piping supports, valve stems, valve hand wheels, etc., shall be arranged such that they do not obstruct 
or interfere with operation and maintenance areas. 
Side access ladders are preferred to front access ladders. 
All piping shall be adequately supported, considering both operating conditions and 
transportation/erection. Piping routing and relevant supports shall be designed to ensure thermal 
expansion and contraction of piping and to avoid sagging, vibration and strain on equipment by means 
of piping stress analysis, where necessary. Whenever required, it shall be possible to adjust supports 
so that they may be set correctly. 
A sufficient number of permanent supports shall be provided at the following locations to prevent 
excessive forces and deflection of piping when flanges are disconnected: 
the inlet and outlet of a safety valve
spectacle blind or blind plates and spacers
equipment nozzles
upstream and downstream of control valves
piping components that must be disconnected or replaced during maintenance
VENDOR shall design the piping within the equipment battery limit and shall provide the maximum 
allowable piping forces and moments, and displacements which may be applied for all battery limit 
connections. As general rule, the piping will be anchored at skid battery limit. 
All connections for auxiliaries (including drain - vent flushing - blanketing) shall be provided at single 
point supply and single point return for each VENDOR skid and located at each baseplate edge. 
Further distribution & combining within skid, if interconnecting piping is in VENDOR’s scope of supply, 
shall be done by VENDOR. In case the interconnecting piping is excluded from VENDOR’s scope of 
supply, VENDOR shall endorse CONTRACTOR’s interconnecting piping routing.

chunks.heading: 10.6 STRUCTURAL
chunks.content:
10.6 STRUCTURAL  
When the equipment shall be delivered mounted on one or several base plates, they shall comply 
with the following requirements: 
a. Unless otherwise specified, base plates shall be constructed of welded structural steel. With
CONTRACTOR’s approval, high strength bolts may be used when welding is impractical;
b. Base plates shall be equipped with lifting lugs or devices suitable for a four (4) point lift and
shall be of sufficient strength and rigidity for transportation and installation to avoid any
distortion or damage to the equipment or internal parts of machinery installed on them during
lifting;
c. The base plate shall be designed to withstand both static and dynamic stresses of machinery
and to transmit equipment generated forces and couples to foundation. Equipment mounted
on the base plate shall not be considered as contributing to its bracing.
d. Base plate shall be equipped with leveling screws for installation on concrete foundation and
alignment screws and shims for equipment alignment.
e. Unless otherwise specified, base plates shall be designed for grouting and shall have the
necessary lipped grouting hole per each bulkhead section and appropriately located vents.
The base plate bottom shall be coated with a suitable coating system compatible for epoxy
grout irrespective of different grouting material followed below skid.
f. The projection of all the equipment included in the skid shall fall within the maximum perimeter
of the base plate.
g. Structures shall be designed to permit approach to process equipment and to lift it without
obstruction.
h. Whenever necessary the skid structure will include the support beams, to support the external
piping. These will be properly designed and installed as per CONTRACTOR comments and
mark-ups.

chunks.content:
11 NOISE

design, for all running conditions, shall be in accordance with specification 4503-SZ-SG-T0000004

power and pressure levels for different octave bands as well as overall A- weighted values, to insert in 
the Noise Source Data sheet format attached in 4503-SZ-SG-T0000004 (SLN-2725-0000-30-S-0001) 
“Noise General Specification”.  
If a part of the supply does not meet the job noise limit, VENDOR shall provide the necessary noise 
attenuation devices (acoustic hoods, acoustic insulation, silencers, etc.) VENDOR shall be responsible 
for the selection of equipment to meet the requirements including modification of design, acoustic 
insulation and provision of enclosures. 
In case acoustic hood is required for noise attenuation, VENDOR shall ensure that all battery limit 
connections are located outside the enclosure. Local instruments shall also protrude outside the 
acoustic enclosure to facilitate reading and replacement. Foundation inputs for design (by VENDOR ) 
must also take into account the provision for installation of acoustic hood. VENDOR shall submit

chunks.heading: 12 PAINTING
chunks.content:
12 PAINTING 
Unless otherwise specified, all equipment and material, Loose components, electric motors, electric 
equipment, control panels, instruments etc. shall be shipped fully protected and painted up to finish 
coat in accordance with specification 4503-VW-SG-T0000001 (SLN-2725-0000-25-S-0001), 
“General Painting Specification ”. 
The extension of painting shall be maximized up to the finish coat for all the prefabricated equipment 
(for example skid mounted packages, pressure vessels, machinery, prefabricated piping, supports, 
etc.), that will require only a final assembly and/or connection at battery limits in the field. 
Only bulk material that will be delivered loose at site and which requires site welding, shall be 
delivered painted with final coat keeping unpainted spaces for welding area. 
Fabricated components shall not be painted until the inspection of the parts is completed .

chunks.heading: 13 INSULATION
chunks.content:
13 INSULATION 
VENDOR shall identify on its PID's and General Arrangement Drawings the parts to be insulated / 
heat traced, even for parts to be insulated / heat traced by others. 
Parts to be insulated shall be designed for the installation of insulation (provision of insulation clips, 
over length of instrument nozzles and valve hand wheels etc.) in accordance with Specification 4503-
XL-SG-T0000001 (SLN-2725-0000-55-S-0004) “Insulation General Specification” .
A
fter the Purchase Order, the VENDOR  shall provide the total length and diameters of lines and 
dimensions of equipment to be insulated and/or heat traced. 
The VENDOR shall ensure to provide adequate space around the piping / equipment to be insulated 
at site by CONTRACTOR, so that dismantling of any component is not required for application of 
insulation. 
Monitoring instruments like pressure gauges, level indicators etc. (switches and transmitters 
excluded) will be insulated in such a way that instrument reading and access for maintenance are not 
hindered.  
Flanged connections and rotating parts (couplings, seal, stuffing boxes etc.) shall not be insulated 
unless specifically required for process reasons, they shall be provided with flanges covers. Valves 
shall be insulated with valve caps for easy removal. 
Insulation shall be applied only after completion of testing. 
VENDOR  shall provide, for equipment insulation, a typical drawing and layout where the exact 
supports position for the external finishing and insulation is indicated. The supports design shall 
guarantee the correct site installation of the insulation system, avoiding any field 
additions/modifications. 
For piping and equipment insulated for personnel protection with metal barrier, the supports shall 
guarantee the strength of the protective structure once it is installed.

chunks.heading: 18.1 PREPARATION FOR SHIPMENT
chunks.content:
18 PROTECTION AND PRESERVATION

18.1 PREPARATION FOR SHIPMENT
It is VENDOR ‘s responsibility to pack the Supply according to the requirements listed in the “Special 
Purchase Conditions” and to properly protect the Supply from any damage and corrosion considering 
also the requirements for sea transportation, truck transportation, railway transportation, different 
handlings and transshipments, transit period and outdoor storage period at Facility site at ambient 
temperature ranging from -38 ºC and +45 ºC. 
The packing shall be seaworthy and suitable for loading by crane, as well as for shipment in freight 
transport, trailers, and trucks and for the transshipment by forklift, in so far as the weight and volume 
of individual packages allow. 
All internal parts other than stainless steel, shall be coated with a rust preservative for outdoor 
storage. 
All openings, flanged or threaded, shall be sealed airtight prior to shipment. Flanges shall be covered 
with gasketed wooden blanks, secured with a minimum of four bolts. Plastic plugs are not acceptable. 
Exposed machined surfaces shall be coated with a thick film of rust preservative and wrapped where 
practical with weather-proof tape. 
All piping and tubing connections shall be tagged or marked for identification in the field. 
All the bearing housings shall be filled with oil or grease prior to shipment. Where it is not feasible, 
VENDOR shall indicate which bearings will have to be lubricated at site. 
Shipping box shall have a removable corrosion protection inspection port. 
VENDOR shall submit his standard preservation and packaging procedure for CONTRACTOR review 
as per the document’s submittal schedule, and at least three months before shipment. 
All the documents including the VENDOR Data Book shall be in final issue at least one month before 
the final inspection.  
No material or equipment shall be shipped until a release note for shipment has been issued by the 
CONTRACTOR.

specified in “Special Purchase Conditions” (for example A= storage in open air, B= storage
under shed, C= Storage in closed dry unheated premises, D= Storage in closed heated
premises, E= Storage in closed air-conditioned premises).

space heater to be energized etc.
c. The periodical inspections/ actions to be performed like rotation of machinery shafts, change
of desiccants etc.
d. The preservation requirements once the equipment has been installed but is not running.
